description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel installation, featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels supported by a single vertical metal pole anchored into a concrete base. The solar panel array consists of six individual modules arranged in a 2x3 grid, with a metallic frame providing structural support. The pole is centrally positioned and appears to be made of galvanized steel, ensuring durability and resistance to weather. The base is embedded in a patch of textured ground, simulating a grassy or earthen surface, which stabilizes the structure. The solar panels are tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture, and the rear of the array shows a supporting frame with visible mounting brackets. No electrical cabling or inverter housing is visible, giving the installation a clean and minimalistic appearance.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with six modules in a 2x3 grid, supported by a single metal pole anchored in a concrete base on a grassy patch. The panels are tilted and framed with metal, with no visible cabling or inverter.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the metallic frame, mounting brackets, and the supporting pole anchored in the ground.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angle of the solar panels and the depth of the supporting pole.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the full 2x3 grid of solar panels and the clean, unobstructed panel surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the panel tilt and the shadow cast by the structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing away from the pole, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, ground installation with a steel post and concrete base
attribute_array_configuration: 2x3 grid of solar panels in a single rectangular array
